aerie: noun. US standard spelling of eyrie.
aim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aimol.
aimer: noun. One who aims; one who is responsible for aiming.
ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
air: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Airoran.
amin: noun. An arbitrator who assessed and collected revenue in the pargana.
amine: noun. A functional group formally derived from ammonia by replacing one, two or three hydrogen atoms with hydrocarbon or other radicals.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
anime: noun. An animated work that originated in Japan, regardless of the artistic style.
are: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Arrernte.
arm: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Armenian.
axe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ayerrerenge.
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
earn: verb. To gain (success, reward, recognition) through applied effort or work.
eme: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emerillon.
emir: noun. A prince, commander or other leader or ruler in an Islamic nation.
enema: noun. An injection of fluid into the large intestine by way of the rectum, usually for medical purposes.
era: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eravallan.
ermine: noun. A weasel found in northern latitudes (Mustela erminea in Eurasia, Alaska, and the Arctic, Mustela haidarum in Haida Gwaii, Mustela
erne: noun. A sea eagle (Haliaeetus), especially the white-tailed eagle (Haliaeetus albicilla)
exam: noun. Clipping of examination, especially when meaning “test” or in compound terms.
examine: verb. To observe carefully or critically to learn about someone or something.
examiner: noun. A person who investigates someone or something.
exine: noun. The outer layer of a pollen grain or spore; the exosporium.
ire: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iresim.
mae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bo-Rukul.
main: adjective. Of chief or leading importance; prime, principal.
man: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mandingo.
mane: noun. Longer hair growth on back of neck of an animal, especially a horse or lion.
mar: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Marathi.
marine: adjective. Belonging to or characteristic of the sea; existing or found in the sea; formed or produced by the sea.
maxi: adjective. Having a hemline at ankle length.
meaner: adjective. comparative form of mean: more mean
meanie: noun. A mean (unkind or miserly) person; a killjoy.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).
mere: adjective. Just, only; no more than, pure and simple, neither more nor better than might be expected.
mine: pronoun. That or those belonging to me.
miner: noun. A person who works in a mine.
minx: noun. A flirtatious, impudent, or pert young woman.
mir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isthmus Mixe.
mire: noun. Deep mud; moist, spongy earth.
mirex: noun. The pesticide and fire retardant perchloropentacyclodecane, which is a persistent organic pollutant.
mix: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mixtepec Mixtec.
mixer: noun. One who, or a device that, mixes or merges things together.
nae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naka'ela.
nam: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngan'gityemerri.
name: noun. Any nounal word or phrase which indicates a particular person, place, class, or thing.
namer: noun. One who names, or calls by name.
nee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kumak.
neem: noun. Azadirachta indica, a large, mostly evergreen tree from India, whose seeds yield neem oil, the insecticide azadirachtin.
nim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nilamba.
nix: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hema.
nixe: noun. Alternative spelling of nixie (“female water-elf”).
rain: noun. Condensed water falling from a cloud.
ram: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Canela.
ramie: noun. A tall, tropical Asian perennial herb, of species Boehmeria nivea, cultivated for its fibrous stems.
ran: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Riantana.
rani: noun. The wife of a rajah.
rax: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rang.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
rei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reli.
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
remain: noun. That which is left; relic; remainder.
reman: verb. To supply with new personnel.
remix: noun. A rearrangement of an older piece of music, possibly including various cosmetic changes.
rename: verb. To give a new name to.
rex: noun. A king, particularly in ancient Rome.
ria: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reang.
rim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nyaturu.
rime: noun. Archaic in the form rimes: originally, any frozen dew forming a white deposit on exposed surfaces; hoar frost (sense 1).
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
xenia: noun. The concept of hospitality to strangers.
